{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/5976","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/5976/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/5976/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/5976/events","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/5976","id":89054626,"node_id":"MDU6SXNzdWU4OTA1NDYyNg==","number":5976,"title":"NpzFile and memmap arrays","user":{"login":"dtheodor","id":1549648,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE1NDk2NDg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1549648?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtheodor","html_url":"https://github.com/dtheodor","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtheodor/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtheodor/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtheodor/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtheodor/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtheodor/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtheodor/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtheodor/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtheodor/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtheodor/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2015-06-17T15:47:26Z","updated_at":"2024-11-27T07:15:33Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hi,

`numpy.load` with memory-mapped file support is great, however the argument `mmap_mode` is silenty ignored when the file being loaded is anything else other than a `.npy` file. I need to combine `numpy.savez_compressed` with loading directly to mem-mapped files.

`numpy.load` returns an `NpzFile` when `.npz` files are loaded.  `NpzFile` is hard-coded to load data directly to numpy arrays. Is it a possibility to pass the `mmap_mode` argument to the initialization of the `NpzFile` and have it restore arrays to a memory-mapped file?
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/5976/reactions","total_count":12,"+1":12,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/5976/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2503086504","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/5976#issuecomment-2503086504","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/5976","id":2503086504,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP86VMhGo","user":{"login":"f0k","id":629706,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYyOTcwNg==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/629706?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/f0k","html_url":"https://github.com/f0k","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/f0k/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/f0k/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/f0k/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/f0k/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/f0k/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/f0k/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/f0k/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/f0k/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/f0k/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-27T07:15:31Z","updated_at":"2024-11-27T07:15:31Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"> If the `.npz` file was created with `np.savez()` (as opposed to `np.savez_compressed()`), then the `.npy` files are actually in there uncompressed. It would be possible to have `NpzFile` return read-only memory maps that point directly into the `.npz` file [...].

Coming back to this, I've turned that recipe into a small Python package, [**mmnpz**](https://github.com/f0k/mmnpz). It allows writing large uncompressed .npz files and loading them as memory maps. I've deviated from my old implementation above in not using file pointers, to be safe for fork()-based multiprocessing, and in explicitly parsing the local file header, so it works for zip files of uncompressed .npy files created by other means than `np.savez()`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2503086504/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

- Added zip support for `np.lib.format.open_memmap`

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jupyter/notebook/issues/5271","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jupyter/notebook","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jupyter/notebook/issues/5271/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jupyter/notebook/issues/5271/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jupyter/notebook/issues/5271/events","html_url":"https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/issues/5271","id":576987478,"node_id":"MDU6SXNzdWU1NzY5ODc0Nzg=","number":5271,"title":"Debugging interactive plots in a Jupyter notebook","user":{"login":"jkitchin","id":1610070,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE2MTAwNzA=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1610070?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jkitchin","html_url":"https://github.com/jkitchin","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jkitchin/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jkitchin/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jkitchin/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jkitchin/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jkitchin/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jkitchin/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jkitchin/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jkitchin/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jkitchin/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2020-03-06T15:02:15Z","updated_at":"2024-05-14T07:41:36Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"The example below should highlight the point that you click on, and change the title of the graph to show the label associated with that point.

If I run this Python script as a script, when I click on a point I will get an error " line 15, in onpick TypeError: only integer scalar arrays can be converted to a scalar index", which is expected. event.ind is a list, and I need to change that to ind = event.ind[0] to be correct here.

However, when I run this in a Jupyter notebook, the figure appears, but the error is silently ignored, so it just appears that the code does not work. Is there a way to get Jupyter to show me that an error has occurred?

```
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

fig, ax = plt.subplots()

x = [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
labels = ['a', 'b', 'c', 'd', 'e', 'f']
ax.plot(x, 'bo', picker=5)

# this is the transparent marker for the selected data point
marker, = ax.plot([0], [0], 'yo', visible=False, alpha=0.8, ms=15)

def onpick(event):
    ind = event.ind
    ax.set_title('Data point {0} is labeled "{1}"'.format(ind, labels[ind]))
    marker.set_visible(True)
    marker.set_xdata(x[ind])
    marker.set_ydata(x[ind])

    ax.figure.canvas.draw()  # this line is critical to change the linewidth

fig.canvas.mpl_connect('pick_event', onpick)

plt.show()
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jupyter/notebook/issues/5271/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jupyter/notebook/issues/5271/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jupyter/notebook/issues/comments/2109498123","html_url":"https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/issues/5271#issuecomment-2109498123","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jupyter/notebook/issues/5271","id":2109498123,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAgGDYc59vGML","user":{"login":"f0k","id":629706,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYyOTcwNg==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/629706?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/f0k","html_url":"https://github.com/f0k","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/f0k/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/f0k/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/f0k/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/f0k/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/f0k/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/f0k/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/f0k/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/f0k/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/f0k/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-05-14T07:41:35Z","updated_at":"2024-05-14T07:41:35Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"This is currently one of the top results for this question in a major search engine. For people from the future: When using jupyterlab with ipympl, then as described [in the ipympl documentation](https://matplotlib.org/ipympl/examples/full-example.html#debugging-widget-updates-and-matplotlib-callbacks), the errors can either be looked at in the jupyterlab log console (View > Show Log Console), or captured via an ipywidgets `widgets.Output`:
```python
out = widgets.Output()
@out.capture()
def onpick(event):
    ...
```
I do not know whether this also works in a classic notebook, which this question was about, but wanted to leave this trace for others.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jupyter/notebook/issues/comments/2109498123/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
